_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.
_what_vs_why: As of v0.283.x: this file records WHY decisions were made (running journal). `docs/PRIORITIES.md` records WHAT comes next (strict-ranked backlog). Both must be updated when scope changes. Don't put 'next action' here — put it in PRIORITIES.md.

Full RFC 5766 (Allocate 401->auth, Refresh, CreatePermission+ChannelBind, stale-nonce; HMAC-SHA1/MD5 long-term cred; ChannelData with Send-indication bootstrap). Best-effort throughout. recent_decisions.why: Operator: 'do it all, my order, no bandaids -- like the theme system.' recent_decisions.decision: GAME-CODE AUDIT (operator redirect: 'see what we have game code feature-wise before filling out massive amounts of data as schema may change'). 3 read-only Explore agents mapped the real runtime state. KEY FINDING (3 agents converged): the game is extensively WRITTEN but barely WIRED. The runtime DataStore is ~99pct empty -- only 5 keys inserted (input_state, camera_*, interaction_prompt). lib.rs:587-589 loads items/plants/recipes CSV via 'let _ = load_csv(...)' and DISCARDS the result; no ItemRegistry/RecipeRegistry/PlantRegistry is ever inserted into the DataStore -> every system that reads data.get(reg) gets None and silently no-ops. Only 6 systems registered+ticked (Time, PlayerController, Interaction, Farming, Inventory, Crafting); ~35 systems (weather, ecology, hydrology, atmosphere, disasters, quests, skills, AI, combat, vehicles, electrical, medical...) exist as real code (some 300-1200 LOC) but are NEVER registered = dead code. Of the 6 ticking: crafting can't craft (no recipe_registry), farming crops freeze (no plant_registry + TimeSystem never EXPORTS game_time to DataStore so all time-dependent systems fall back to Spring/t=0), player ECS-physics path dead (physics_world never in DataStore), interaction E-key only logs. The ONE genuinely-working gameplay system is Inventory (and it works by parsing items.csv DIRECTLY, bypassing the dead registry). STATUS.md/FEATURES.md OVERSTATE -- mark crafting/farming/weather/quests done when they're no-op/unregistered. CONCLUSION: operator's instinct right -- pouring data would be wasted (nothing consumes it; schemas shift as wiring forces real shape). REAL PRIORITY = WIRE THE ENGINE, holistically + enforced (theme-system standard): a consistent data-loading layer + a lint that fails the build on orphaned data / implemented-but-unregistered systems (the theme_token_lint analog), NOT a 3-registry bandaid. ARC ORDER: land TURN (done) -> merge container 6c8d8e5f (its container_registry IS correctly wired = the template) -> merge MMO de2fcf54 (reconcile Cargo.toml tokio signal vs sha1/md-5) -> engine-wiring arc (registries->DataStore + game_time export + register/feed systems + physics bridge + interaction dispatch + enforcement lint) -> reconcile docs to reality -> THEN data-fill (finally has consumers). Full per-domain audit tables in this session transcript.
recent_decisions.why: Operator explicitly redirected from data-breadth toward understanding code first; the audit proved data had already outrun working code (the container subagent's earlier item/recipe-registry-not-wired finding generalizes to the whole engine). Doing the wiring as an enforced architecture (not a patch) is the operator's 'no bandaids, like the theme/widgets system' bar -- a lint makes the fix non-regressable.

recent_decisions.decision: Concurrency arc continued (v0.307.0 + v0.308.0) + velocity directive persisted. Operator gave an emphatic working-norm directive: stop producing checkpoints/recaps/permission-asks, do EVERYTHING, move as fast as possible, no arbitrary delays except them being away from the keyboard, recaps come from logs not replies. Persisted to CLAUDE.md 'Working norm' + new memory feedback_velocity_no_checkpoints.md (correctness still required — 'fast' = no artificial stops, not skipping verification; cost is a flat Max sub with abundant headroom so lean on parallel agents). Then shipped, parallel (me on client + background agents on relay, split by feature-flag-disjoint trees so neither breaks the other's compile): v0.307.0 = PIN-unlock off-thread (mirrors v0.306 passphrase-unlock) + clipboard image upload off-thread (grab on main, upload POST on worker) + SQLite read-pool FOUNDATION (r2d2 8 read-only conns + opt-in with_read_conn; with_conn/with_conn_mut unchanged on the single writer; agent audited that with_conn is pervasively used for writes so kept them on the writer — the conservative correct call). v0.308.0 = migrated 57 provably-read-only hot paths to with_read_conn (DM list/load, members, signed-object reads, channel/message history, roles list, profiles, reactions, DID resolve). VERIFIED each agent's work before ship: reviewed diffs, ran the full suite (279/279), backstop-grepped for write-SQL inside any with_read_conn closure (none in production — only the deliberate rejection test). The read-only pool physically rejects mis-migrated writes (loud, not silent) — strong safety net. Correctly LEFT on the writer: all read-then-write closures + role_def (default-deny on pool-exhaustion would silently demote a capability check). EXE: build-game batched after the relay run (server-side changes redeploy via CI; one exe sync at the end of the concurrency batch).
recent_decisions.why: Operator wants maximum forward velocity with no artificial stops, but still requires double-checking subagent work. Resolved by parallelizing across feature-flag-disjoint trees (gui builds exclude relay and vice versa, so a background relay agent and my client edits can't break each other's compile), and by leaning on the read-only-pool's physical write-rejection + the 279-test gate as the verification backbone for the DB-layer changes (the highest-risk surface). Batched the exe build because relay-only changes have no native-visible effect (desktop uses the VPS relay) — building an exe per relay release would be an arbitrary delay with no operator-testable payoff; one build-game after the relay batch keeps the local exe in sync per SOP.

recent_decisions.decision: Parallel concurrency batch (v0.306.0): vault-unlock off-thread (me) + relay verify-offload (background agent), both verified before merge. Operator: 'work on what you can next in parallel, then tell me exactly what to test.' Split the work so the two fronts CANNOT collide: I did C1 vault-unlock in src/gui/ while a background agent did R1+R2 relay verify-offload in src/relay/ — and crucially the agent builds `--features relay` which EXCLUDES the gui, so neither side could break the other's compile. C1 (src/gui/widgets/passphrase_modal.rs + mod.rs): the 600k-iter PBKDF2 decrypt (+ the legacy 100k→600k re-encrypt, a 2nd heavy PBKDF2) now run on a worker thread with an 'Unlocking…' spinner; cheap post-steps (keychain stash, apply_pq_identity, save) stay on main; new apply_unlock_outcome() holds the post-decrypt logic verbatim; new GuiState passphrase_unlocking + passphrase_unlock_rx (native-gated, mirrors p2p_group_loader). Covers the main unlock path; draw_change/draw_pin_unlock/draw_pin_change still inline (tracked follow-up). R1+R2 (agent, src/relay/api_v2_objects.rs + relay.rs): object-ingest + chat-message ML-DSA verify moved to spawn_blocking; the DUPLICATE ingest verify removed (post_object verified then put_signed_object verified again). I VERIFIED the agent's work before merge (not on trust): reviewed both diffs; confirmed put_signed_object verifies FIRST and returns Err before any INSERT (signed_objects.rs:72) so bad-sig is never stored; confirmed the IngestError status-code classification matches the real error-message markers ('signature verification failed' → 401, 'author_pubkey must be' → 400); confirmed chat verify is .await.unwrap_or(false) = FAIL CLOSED. Integrated tree: native compiles, 277/277 relay tests pass (incl. put_rejects_tampered_object + identify_challenge + federation forged/tampered rejection), 6/6 pbkdf2 round-trip tests pass, theme/glyph lints green. Shipped v0.306.0; local exe v0.306.1; relay redeploys via CI. concurrency-roadmap.md updated (C1, R1, R2 → done; tails tracked).
recent_decisions.why: The operator asked for parallel progress + a test list. Splitting by feature-flag-disjoint trees (gui vs relay) let me genuinely run a background agent while I worked, with zero compile-collision risk (relay build excludes gui). Took the security-critical vault-unlock myself; let the agent do the (also careful but more mechanical) relay async-offload, then verified it the way the operator requires — which confirmed the agent preserved every security gate (bad-sig rejection, fail-closed chat verify, status codes). Both are 'move existing-correct logic to a different execution context' = high-value, low-logic-risk, strongly test-covered. Vault-unlock PIN/change flows + the lower-volume relay verify sites are deferred tails (tracked in concurrency-roadmap.md), not rushed.

recent_decisions.decision: BUDGET MODEL CORRECTED + first verified parallel-implementation batch (v0.305.0). Operator corrected a stale assumption I'd cited TWICE as a reason to scope down: they're on Claude Max FLAT $100/mo, had used only ~6% of the weekly allotment (resetting within the hour) — abundant headroom; usage is a subscription rate-limit, NOT per-token dollars. Updated memory project_financial_context.md: stop citing 'token/agent cost / your budget' as a scoping reason; the real gates on 'do everything' are (a) correctness/verifiability (I ship only what I can double-check; visual/UI still needs operator eyes) and (b) file-conflict discipline (parallel agents need disjoint scope / worktrees). Kept the separate long-term 'architect for Claude's eventual absence' principle (different timescale). THEN acted on the standing 'do everything incl backlog' with cost cleared: launched 2 concurrent scoped subagents on DISJOINT files — (1) docs-only: refreshed the 200-versions-stale STATUS.md (v0.132→v0.304, reframed as inventory deferring to PRIORITIES, added a crypto reality-check matching CLAUDE.md) + swept dead pre-v0.90 path refs across 13 docs; (2) tests-only: 11 relay storage integration tests (DM envelope/roundtrip, mutual-follow DM gate, channel persistence). VERIFIED everything before shipping (operator's rule): confirmed only-expected-files changed (no scope violations), 277/277 relay tests pass, native compiles, STATUS crypto claims match canonical. The tests agent surfaced a REAL user-facing bug — get_dm_conversations counted MY OUTBOUND DMs as unread instead of the partner's inbound (placeholder ?1..?M=my_ph/to_key, ?M+1..=p_ph/from_key, but bound partner-keys-first → inverted from/to). I confirmed it by walking the numbered-placeholder binding, FIXED it (bind my-keys-first, matching the last_msg query above), and tightened the test to assert unread=2 (regression lock). Also fixed FEATURES.md DM crypto drift (ECDH→Kyber768). Shipped v0.305.0; local exe v0.305.1. The concurrent agents shared the working tree (no worktree isolation) but disjoint scopes held — Agent1 even observed Agent2's in-progress .rs writes and correctly left them alone.
recent_decisions.why: The budget correction is a durable operator correction → persisted immediately to memory (the cross-session rule). With cost off the table, the right move is MORE parallelism + MORE verification, not less — but bounded to autonomously-verifiable work (docs I cross-check, tests I run); visual UI (bloom, glossary widget) still deferred for operator eyes. Verifying the subagent output rather than trusting it caught the agents' good work AND let me upgrade a 'documented bug' into a shipped fix + regression test — exactly the double-check the operator asked for, and the higher-value outcome (a real DM-unread-badge bug fixed) than just adding tests. Deferred (own careful passes, tracked in docs/design/concurrency-roadmap.md): vault-unlock PBKDF2 off-thread (C1, worst freeze), relay verify→spawn_blocking + SQLite read-pool (R1-R4, live concurrency surface).

recent_decisions.decision: Multithreading audit (4 parallel read-only agents) + first client wins (v0.304.0). Operator asked 'what else can we multithread' then 'do everything incl. backlog, spin up subagents, double-check before upload.' Pushed back (candor) on literal 'everything': blind whole-backlog fan-out can't be verified to the no-bandaids bar, parallel agents editing shared files (GuiState/mod.rs) cause the context-rot corruption CLAUDE.md warns about, and a speculative fan-out is real $ on a tight budget. Instead ran a SAFE parallel READ-ONLY audit: 4 general-purpose agents mapped (1) UI-thread blockers, (2) engine parallelism, (3) relay concurrency, (4) backlog triage — findings captured in NEW docs/design/concurrency-roadmap.md (file:line grounded, so the audit never re-runs). Then shipped the client-only low-risk subset v0.304.0: group message SEND now backgrounds the POST (was blocking every send) + optimistic echo; killed a per-poll Dilithium+Kyber keygen in replace_p2p_messages (profile_public_key already IS the dilithium hex → cheap hex+blake3); removed a dead synchronous first-render refresh_p2p_groups (the v0.303.0 background list refresh preempts it). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED with reasons: vault-unlock PBKDF2 off-thread (C1 — worst freeze ~200ms-1s but security-critical login path → own tested pass); relay verify→spawn_blocking + duplicate-verify removal + SQLite read-pool (R1-R4 — live security/concurrency surface → own tested passes, NOT rushed into a mixed turn); engine/terrain rayon parallelism (Tier-2 — audit proved it's LATENT: heavy systems aren't wired into the live runner + terrain isn't sampled per-vertex live, so it'd optimize code that doesn't run = premature). cargo native+relay green, lints green, group_e2ee 2/2, KAT pass. Backlog triage: 10 safe-autonomous / 7 need-operator-decision / 15 multi-week / 6 blocked (see roadmap doc + the audit).
recent_decisions.why: The operator's 'double-check before uploading' is the constraint that makes 'everything at once' self-limiting — I can only ship what I can verify to this project's bar. So: parallelize the SAFE part (read-only audit, no file conflicts, far cheaper than implementation fan-out), bank the unambiguous client wins, and route the security/concurrency-sensitive + live-server items to their own careful passes rather than a risky mega-commit. Tier-2 engine parallelism is correctly deferred because parallelizing not-yet-live code is premature optimization (measure first). This protects the operator's budget AND the mission's correctness bar while still moving fast on what's verifiable.

recent_decisions.decision: Native P2P groups: async + cog menu + auto-switch on create (v0.303.0). Four operator issues from testing v0.302.x. (1) 'Can't leave/disband in app' — ROOT CAUSE was UI overflow, NOT a crypto/auth bug (verified subject==author for self-leave: derive_pq_identity and submit_signed_object use the identical dilithium derivation, so the relay authorizes it). The Leave/Disband ghost buttons were crammed into the group header row (Back + name + 'end-to-end encrypted' + Copy invite + Leave + Disband) and clipped off the right edge on a narrow center panel. FIX: moved Copy invite / Leave / Disband into a COG + right-click popup on the left-rail P2P row (egui popup_below_widget, deferred-action vars applied after the loop) — mirrors the web right-click menu AND the legacy native group cog AND the operator's original 'cog→settings popup' mental model. Header trimmed to Back + name + encrypted + Copy invite. (2) 'App didn't update when I disbanded/left via web (still 2 users, chat persisted)' — native never refreshed the list/roster after initial load. FIX: background list refresh every ~6s (spawn_groups_list_refresh) keeps the rail + counts fresh; if the open group vanished (disbanded/left elsewhere) it exits to #general + drops the decrypted history. (3) 'Switching groups hangs ~1s, web instant' — the load did 4-6 blocking ureq calls on the click frame. FIX: ALL group net/crypto now on a BACKGROUND THREAD — api_v2::load_group_blocking returns a GroupLoad struct over an mpsc channel; drain_p2p_loaders applies it on the main thread. Click switches the view INSTANTLY (header + 'Loading…'); messages fill in when the worker returns; the ~4s periodic reload no longer freezes. Replaced synchronous enter_p2p_group/poll_p2p_group with spawn_group_load/apply_group_load/spawn_groups_list_refresh/drain_p2p_loaders. New native-gated GuiState: p2p_group_loader (String,Receiver<GroupLoad>), p2p_groups_list_loader (Receiver<Vec<P2pGroupInfo>>), p2p_group_loading bool. (4) 'Auto-switch to group on create to force keygen' — both clients now switch into the new group after create (native create modal + web createP2pGroup→openP2pGroup). cargo check native+relay green; theme+glyph lints green; web syntax OK; NO relay/schema change. Shipped v0.303.0 (CI auto-deploys web on push); local exe v0.303.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator hit the hang + couldn't reach leave/disband + saw stale state after cross-client changes. The hang and the staleness share one root cause — synchronous ureq on the UI thread — so the correct fix is to move group I/O off-thread (mpsc + try_recv, the standard egui async pattern), which fixes BOTH and brings native to web parity (instant switch). Diagnosed the leave/disband 'bug' as header overflow, NOT broken crypto (ruled out by confirming subject==author) — so the fix is UI placement (a cog/right-click popup), which also matches how the operator described it originally and how web + legacy native already work. Chose threading over the cheaper 'defer enter to next frame' because the operator explicitly wants web-parity instant + the periodic refresh must not freeze either — do-it-right over a patch.

recent_decisions.decision: Crown icon on owned groups (v0.302.0). Native: new paint_crown in widgets/icons.rs (filled band rect + 3 convex triangle spikes — a concave crown polygon won't tessellate; takes a color param) painted at the left of creator rows in theme.warning() (the gold token — keeps theme_token_lint green, no hardcoded literal). Verified LIVE via curl on the deployed icons.js + chat-social.js?v=7. Both lints green. Also: confirmed v0.301.0 (leave/disband relay + disbanded migration) deployed LIVE via the CI pipeline (deploy.yml rsyncs web + rebuilds/restarts relay on every push; the v0.301.0 run succeeded 3m40s, so the migration ran on relay boot). My manual `just sync` was redundant with CI. Local exe v0.302.1.
recent_decisions.why: Small directed UX ask, done cleanly: reused the existing is_creator signal (no new endpoint), and crucially used the theme's gold token (theme.warning / --warning) rather than a hardcoded crown color, so it stays themeable and doesn't grow the theme_token_lint debt — the disciplined choice the lint exists to enforce. Verified live by fetching the public assets (read-only), NOT by querying prod (an ad-hoc prod SSH read was correctly blocked by the auto-mode classifier as out-of-scope; the project's own `just status` + a public-asset curl gave the same confirmation safely).

recent_decisions.decision: Delete-groups shipped both clients (v0.301.0): Leave (anyone) + Disband (creator). Operator confirmed P2P groups work end-to-end (created on app+web, cross-invited, chatted both ways) then asked for a way to delete groups (list only grew). Built it sovereign-signed-object style, NOT a relay RPC: (1) LEAVE — relaxed index_group_member authorization in groups_p2p.rs so a member may action='remove' THEMSELVES (subject == author) even if not the creator; reuses the existing group_member_v1 type (no new wire format). Web buildGroupMemberV1 already existed → leaveP2pGroup; native submit_group_leave. (2) DISBAND — new group_disband_v1 signed object; index_group_disband verifies author == creator and sets a new p2p_groups.disbanded flag; p2p_groups_for_member filters disbanded=0 so it drops off EVERY member's list. The signed object is the durable P2P-replicable tombstone; index_group uses INSERT OR IGNORE so re-indexing group_v1 never un-disbands. Schema: disbanded added to CREATE (fresh) + guarded idempotent ALTER (live DB already had p2p_groups; DEFAULT 0 keeps existing groups live — followed the CLAUDE.md migration-ordering lesson). (3) CREATOR-AWARENESS — GET /api/v2/groups now returns is_creator per group (server-computed via new p2p_group_creator_fp helper + author_fingerprint of the caller) so Disband shows ONLY to the creator (never a silent no-op); native P2pGroupInfo gained is_creator. UI: web right-click menu (Leave / Disband-for-everyone, both confirm); native header Leave + Disband buttons next to Copy invite; both return to #general + refresh the list on action. Wired index_group_disband into put_signed_object. 4 new relay tests (self-leave drops member + group still exists for creator; non-creator can't evict someone else; creator disband hides for all; non-creator disband ignored) → 13/13 groups_p2p. group_e2ee 2/2, KAT PASS, native+relay compile. Relay change → minor bump v0.301.0; `just sync` rebuilt the VPS; local exe v0.301.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator's explicit ask ('no way to delete groups, list getting bigger'). Did it RIGHT per the mission norm: a group is a set of signed objects, so leave/disband must also be signed objects that replicate P2P — not a relay-only delete that would vanish when the relay is bypassed (Phase 3+). Leave reuses group_member_v1 (minimal surface); disband is a new tombstone type. Self-leave authorization (subject==author) is the clean rule: you can always exit a group you're in, no creator needed. is_creator computed server-side avoids an extra per-group fetch on the client AND prevents the bad UX of showing Disband to someone whose disband the relay would silently ignore. Deferred the epoch-key-bootstrap polish (creator auto-rekey from the group LIST so a fresh joiner doesn't see 'No epoch key yet' until the creator opens the group) — it self-heals today and is a smoothness nicety, not a correctness bug; noted for later.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P group conversation is now a CHANNEL, not a modal — both clients (web v0.299.2, native v0.300.0). Operator tested v0.299.x and reported: (web) clicking a group hangs ~2s then opens a modal that lands behind its own darkening backdrop (unclickable), and 'why are we popping up an entirely new chat system? switching from #general to a group should be like changing #general → #announcements'; ALSO (web) groups don't appear on first load, only after interacting; (native) same modal problem + it's a separate chat UI. FIXES: (1) WEB — killed openP2pGroupDialog modal; new openP2pGroup(id,name) switches the main chat center panel to the group like switchChannel does (window.activeP2pGroup; messages render via the standard addChatMessage; composer monkey-patch routes sends to the group; switchChannel/openDmConversation/openGroup patched to closeP2pGroup; 4s poll). Invite via header link OR right-click context menu (small dismiss-on-outside popover, no full-bleed backdrop). View switch is instant (network load runs in background after the switch). (2) WEB first-load bug — loadP2pGroups set _p2pGroupsFetched=true BEFORE the myKey guard, so if it ran pre-identity the flag stuck true and never retried until create/join. Fixed: flag set inside loadP2pGroups only on a real attempt; renderGroupList gates the lazy trigger on myKey; connect() proactively loads groups after identity ready. (3) NATIVE — removed draw_p2p_group_modal entirely; P2P group = new chat_active_channel prefix 'p2pgroup:<id>' joining the existing dm:/group:/scratchpad dispatch. enter_p2p_group (full sync on click: rekey-if-creator + epoch fetch + roster fp→name/key map + decrypt) and poll_p2p_group (light 4s re-decrypt) project GroupMessages into chat_messages tagged with the channel so the standard renderer handles them (identicons/grouping/theme reused). Header: Back + green name + 'end-to-end encrypted' label + 'Copy invite' button (mints 7-day ticket to clipboard — no modal). Composer routes p2pgroup sends over HTTP (group_msg_v1) gated OUT of the WS block, with optimistic echo. Left-rail rows highlight active; empty state shows no-key/no-msg hint not the raw channel id. Dropped 🔒 (U+1F512 tofus in egui font) → added to icon_glyph_lint BROKEN_GLYPHS; E2EE signalled in words. State churn (mod.rs): removed show_p2p_group_modal/p2p_group_open_id/p2p_group_modal_ticket/p2p_group_chat_messages/p2p_group_chat_compose; added p2p_group_active_id/last_fetch/fp_to_key/fp_to_name/invite_status; kept epoch+epoch_key. cargo check native+relay green; group_e2ee 2/2; group-object KAT PASS; icon_glyph + theme lints green. Shipped v0.300.0; local exe v0.300.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator was explicit twice: the group chat must be the SAME interface as server channels (switch like #general → #announcements), not a bolt-on modal with a parallel chat system — and the modal kept hitting an egui-style z-order bug where the backdrop covered it. Removing every full-bleed darkening overlay (web AND native) eliminates the bug CLASS rather than patching it. The native fix mirrors the web one structurally (a group is just another active-channel prefix), so the two clients now share one mental model. The first-load fix was a real correctness bug (flag burned before identity). Deferred: native enter does redundant blocking HTTP (brief hitch) — left as a noted follow-up rather than threading it now, because correctness + the inline UX were the operator's asks; threading is an optimization to do only if the hitch annoys in testing.

recent_decisions.decision: Phase 2 cross-client COMPLETE (v0.299.0) — both clients can now interop end-to-end on the same group object. Two pieces landed in one ship: (1) web rekey-on-join: chat-groups-p2p.js rekeyIfCreatorNeeds — when the creator opens a group dialog and the roster has new members not covered by the current epoch_key recipients, auto-mint a fresh epoch sealed to the full roster (forward secrecy on rotation). Wired into refresh() before the epoch-key fetch. parseGroupEpochKeyPayload exposed in pq-object.js to read existing recipients. (2) native chat-in-groups: new src/net/group_e2ee.rs (epoch-key sealing via ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM, AES-GCM per message — byte-identical to web's pq-object.js helpers; 2 round-trip tests pass: group_e2ee_roundtrip + wrong_recipient_cannot_open_epoch_key). src/net/api_v2.rs gains submit_group_epoch_key_v1 + submit_group_msg_v1 + GroupMessage struct + fetch_group_epoch_payload + fetch_and_decrypt_group_messages + fetch_group_members + fetch_signed_object + rekey_if_creator_needs + fetch_my_epoch_key + create_group_and_first_invite now issues the initial epoch key sealed to creator. chat.rs draw_p2p_group_modal: full chat view (ScrollArea message list with You/short-hex labels + UTC time, TextEdit compose + Send button + Enter-to-send + refresh button); dialog open triggers a sync rekey + key fetch + decrypt. GuiState fields: p2p_group_chat_epoch, p2p_group_chat_epoch_key, p2p_group_chat_messages, p2p_group_chat_compose. KAT still locked (group-object-kat: PASS). cargo check --features native + --features relay both green. Shipped: v0.299.0 (Rust changes minor bump). Local exe: v0.299.1 (just build-game). Phase 2 is done; Phases 3-5 are the resilience tracks (P2P transport, relay-independence, mDNS/DHT) and don't block testability.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: finish implementing the features that have been discussed before I can properly test groups. The MUST items were (1) cross-identity test path (rekey-on-join, or two web identities can't read each other) and (2) native chat-in-groups (so the operator can actually test on his native build, not just web). Both shipped together because the new Rust crypto module group_e2ee.rs deserves to land with the UI it serves, and rekey + initial-epoch + send/receive are all the same loop. Polish items (right-click context menu; signed leave/disband) are deferred — they're cleanup, not blockers for the test loop.

recent_decisions.decision: PRODUCTION INCIDENT + HOTFIX v0.262.2. Relay crash-looped (nginx 502, total outage; Deploy-to-VPS exit 3). Ground truth via ssh humanity-vps journalctl: panic src/relay/mod.rs:141 "table roles has no column named can_image_share". ROOT CAUSE: v0.261 (worsened v0.262) added new columns to BOTH the roles CREATE TABLE and the seed INSERT, but the seed ran BEFORE the idempotent ALTERs. Fresh DBs: CREATE makes the cols → every fresh-DB test passed → bug shipped. Live relay.db: CREATE IF NOT EXISTS no-op (old 10-col table) → seed INSERT references missing column → Storage::open expect() panic → systemd crash-loop. The upgrade path was NEVER tested. FIX: moved both guarded ALTER blocks (v0.261 image/file + v0.262 R4 + backfill) to run BEFORE the seed loop; seed is now INSERT OR IGNORE post-ALTER (correct on fresh AND upgrade). Added regression test upgrade_from_pre_v0261_roles_schema_does_not_panic (rewinds roles to pre-v0.261 shape, reopens Storage — panics pre-fix, clean post-fix). 6 roles tests + 3 lints + relay build green. LESSON: any migration that adds columns to a CREATE TABLE must add them via idempotent ALTER too, and the ALTERs must precede any seed/INSERT that names them; ALWAYS add an upgrade-path test (rewind schema + reopen) — fresh-DB tests cannot catch ordering bugs.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings centering attempt 3 (v0.258) — the one that should actually work. v0.253 vertical_centered+set_max_width and v0.257 horizontal+add_space+allocate_ui_with_layout BOTH failed (operator screenshot on v0.257.1 confirmed section still full-bleed-left). Also de-cramped the policy matrix + roles grids (column spacing spacing_lg/md -> spacing_xl, min_col_width on the matrix). Was candid with operator: this is attempt 3, using the codebase-proven primitive not more theory; asked for one more screenshot.

recent_decisions.decision: PQ Increment 3 SHIPPED (v0.253) — gated PQ-signature ENFORCEMENT. New server_settings.require_pq_signatures bool (default OFF; full positional-SQL wiring: struct/Default/get ?22/set ?23/CREATE+idempotent ALTER; round-trip test locks it). Relay Chat handler: Inc2 soft-log replaced with a (has_pq_key, sent_pq_sig, require_pq) match — PQ key+valid=OK; PQ key+invalid/missing & require ON = REJECT (Private actionable error + continue, no broadcast); require OFF = Inc2 soft; NO pq key on file = NEVER enforced (the safety valve — old/incapable clients never lock out, auto-upgrade on reconnect). ServerSettingsUpdate gained require_pq_signatures Option<bool> + handler apply. Native Server Settings ADMIN policy got the checkbox + a thorough body_hint (turn on after pq_dualsign telemetry shows full adoption; reversible).
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: Do it, get accounts off the backlog. Candid engineering call: "make PQ canonical" done SAFELY = enforce the PQ sig (the actual quantum-forgery defense), NOT re-key every Ed25519-keyed table (catastrophic, orphans data, never right at billions-scale). Ed25519 stays the storage key + Solana wallet; the existing Ed25519<->Dilithium map + an enforcement gate IS the correct identity-migration pattern (resolution indirection, not primary-key rewrite). Toggle default-OFF + never-enforce-uncapable-accounts makes the cutover a safe, reversible, informed 1-click op instead of a deploy-day lockout. Pushed back on the implicit big-bang while delivering the real security goal.

recent_decisions.decision:
PQ Increment 2 SHIPPED (v0.252) — dual-sign, soft/log-only. Chat messages now carry pq_signature (Dilithium3 sig over the IDENTICAL preimage as Ed25519: content
timestamp). crypto.js pqSignChatMessage; 4 chat-messages.js send sites attach it. Relay Chat handler extracts pq_signature from the raw inbound JSON Value (NO RelayMessage::Chat enum/constructor change — avoided the ~20-site ripple; soft mode does not persist/rebroadcast it), and when the sender dilithium_public is on file, verify_dilithium_signature (new, broadcast.rs, mirrors verify_ed25519_signature, same preimage) → tracing target pq_dualsign PQ-OK/PQ-MISMATCH. Ed25519 stays authoritative; PQ result NEVER rejects/alters broadcast. New cross-impl KAT: dilithium_js_signature_verifies_in_rust embeds a frozen noble-produced 3309-byte sig fixture (src/relay/core/pq_kat_dilithium_sig.hex) and asserts RustCrypto verify_dilithium accepts it + rejects tampered (ML-DSA sign is hedged/non-deterministic so we freeze a valid sig; verify is deterministic). 12 pq_crypto tests + JS pq-kat + 3 lints + native/relay all green.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: do what you believe best (3rd autonomy grant) + wants PQ momentum. Inc2 is safe/additive (soft verify = zero enforcement = zero break even if a client preimage were wrong — just telemetry). Extracting pq_signature from raw JSON instead of typing it into the enum was the key scope/risk decision: Chat is constructed in ~20 places; a typed field would force editing them all. STOPPING after Inc2 — Inc3 (canonical flip + Ed25519->PQ alias/migration so no data orphaned) is the catastrophic-if-wrong step and explicitly needs operator sign-off + its own session.

recent_decisions.decision: PQ migration Increment 1 SHIPPED (v0.251) — additive dual-key foundation. Chat client now derives a Dilithium3 keypair from the SAME 32-byte BIP39 seed as Ed25519 (web/chat/pq.js + attachPqIdentity in crypto.js) and presents dilithium_public on the identify message; relay records it in nullable registered_names.dilithium_public alongside Ed25519. Ed25519 stays the canonical primary key — NOTHING canonical changed. Mirrors the ecdh_public precedent end-to-end (migration/storage/enum/handler/Peer/PeerInfo/UserInfo/PeerJoined). CRITICAL correctness work: discovered noble@2.x blake3 derive-key context must be UTF-8 BYTES not a string (existing pq-identity.js passes a string → that federation PQ path was never exercised / is broken) AND noble@0.6.x ml-dsa API is sign(msg,sk)/verify(sig,msg,pk) (pq-identity.js has the old wrong order too). Established a cross-language KAT: fixed master 32x0x07 -> dil_seed f0dfc6e8.. -> pk blake3 3f4ff5c7.. , asserted in BOTH src/relay/core/pq_crypto.rs::dilithium_cross_language_kat AND scripts/pq-kat.mjs (just pq-kat) against the actual vendored artifact. Verified noble ml-dsa-65 == RustCrypto ml-dsa byte-for-byte. noble VENDORED same-origin (web/shared/vendor/noble-pq.bundle.js, 58KB self-contained ESM, provenance header + sha256, reproducible via scripts/build-noble-bundle.mjs / just pq-vendor) — no CDN for a primary-identity dep; CSP script-src self already covers it. Graceful fallback everywhere (no PQ -> exact pre-PQ Ed25519 behavior). Storage round-trip test added. cargo native+relay + 3 lint tests + both KATs green.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: go full PQ, do it right from the start. Pushed back candidly that the canonical flip must be per-user-as-they-reconnect (clients update asynchronously; everything keyed by Ed25519 hex) — so it ships across several dependency-ordered releases, Inc1 being the mandatory additive foundation (builds the Ed25519->Dilithium map). Vendoring (vs CDN allowlist) chosen because a primary-identity crypto dep must not depend on a third party — that IS doing it right. KAT-locked cross-language because a silent JS/Rust derivation mismatch = unrecoverable identity corruption.

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d-quad shipped (v0.248): (1) eclipse shadow-axis ground point — subpoint_lat_lon_deg() converts the Earth->Moon ecliptic vector to geographic lat/lon using J2000 mean obliquity (23.4393 deg physical constant) + the IAU GMST polynomial; both exact in the model J2000 time base. Honest approximation (sub-lunar point, ignores parallax/oblateness/dT) — labeled approx in the HUD. (2) Forward Sky-Events scan — scan_upcoming_sky_events() steps sim_time 3h for 400 days, detecting eclipses by enter/exit windows (refined to 1-min peak) and tight (<1deg) conjunctions/occultations by per-pair local-minimum-separation turning points (refined). Cached in GuiState cosmos_upcoming_* with a 12h-drift + 300ms-wall throttle so the ~3000-sample scan never runs per-frame. Validated by 3 unit tests incl. sub-solar latitude must hit +/-23.44 over a year (pins obliquity sign+magnitude; celestial pole P=(0,cose,sine) derived for the model Y-up/XZ-ecliptic frame).

recent_decisions.decision: Closed the name-only-kick admin-protection security gap (v0.247). handle_mod_action previously only ran the "non-admin cannot act on an admin" check when an explicit target KEY was supplied; the name-only path (target empty, target_name set) skipped it entirely — a rogue mod could kick/ban an admin by name. Fix: resolve candidate_keys = [target key if any] + keys_for_name(target_name) (dedup), then run BOTH protections against the whole set: (a) self-action guard now catches name-only self-target (and extended to mute), (b) admin/owner protection refuses a non-admin if ANY resolved registration is admin/owner. Also widened protected set from admin-only to admin OR owner.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list security item. The TODO had said "currently fine because only admins use moderation UI in practice" — that is a trust assumption, not a control. Mission = civilization-scale, billions of users, AI agents as mods; the control must hold without assuming who uses the UI. Self-mute now blocked too (footgun: a mod muting themselves has no easy modal self-recovery).

recent_decisions.decision: Mute reworked off the role bandaid (v0.246). The OLD /mute (broadcast.rs handle_mod_command) set user_roles.role=muted — clobbering the real role (donor/verified/mod) — and /unmute reset role=user (a non-existent role -> default-denies to unverified). Data loss: mute+unmute a Donor and they came back unverified. The modal Mute (mod_action mute) was outright unimplemented (returned not-implemented-use-kick). Built a muted_members table ORTHOGONAL to roles: is_muted/mute_user/unmute_user/list_muted + MutedUser struct (mirrors banned_keys/BannedUser). unmute_user also clears any LEGACY role=muted row, best-effort reset to unverified (their original role was already lost by the old bandaid). relay.rs Chat handler now gates on role==muted OR is_muted() (legacy honored). New WS: MutedListRequest/MutedList{target}/Unmute (mod-gated; MutedList targeted via send-loop filter, never leaks). mod_action mute|unmute implemented (multi-key via keys_for_name, notifies actor + target). Slash /mute /unmute now call mute_user/unmute_user (no role clobber). New MOD-section Server Settings > Muted users panel (per-row Unmute, mirrors Banned users). Mute stays mod-level; the existing admin-protection check (any action on an admin by non-admin is refused) already covers mute-an-admin.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item: muted_members table for Mute. Doing it as a separate table (not role=muted) is the no-bandaid choice — mute must not destroy role state. Mirrored the v0.245 ban architecture 1:1 for consistency (same panel pattern, same targeted-WS-delivery, same chrono-free date fmt).

recent_decisions.decision: Full ban management (v0.245). The modal Ban button (handle_mod_action) never persisted to banned_keys — Ban silently == Kick. Wired it: ban_user(key,name) inserts into banned_keys, force-closes live sockets (kicked_keys + peers.remove for BOTH kick and ban now), and the identify gate (relay.rs:2320, already existed) rejects banned keys on reconnect. CRITICAL discovery: the kick path deletes the user from registered_names, so /unban <name> (resolves via keys_for_name) could no longer find a key — a ban was IRREVERSIBLE. Fix per no-bandaids rule: banned_keys gained a name column (idempotent ALTER + fresh CREATE), new storage BannedUser struct + list_banned()/ban_user()/unban_user() (set_banned kept as name-less delegator for slash /ban /unban back-comat). New WS: BannedListRequest{} (admin-gated), BannedList{users,target:Option} (targeted to requesting admin via send-loop filter so the ban list never broadcasts to non-admins), Unban{target}. New Server Settings > ADMIN > Banned users panel: auto-requests once/session, Refresh button, per-row name/short-key/date + Unban. chat_banned_users + chat_banned_requested GuiState fields; chat_banned_requested reset on ws disconnect. recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item "banned_keys wire-up so Ban prevents rejoin". A moderation feature that silently half-works is exactly the kind of bandaid the mission doc forbids. Built the full reversible loop (ban persists + unban UI) rather than just the insert, because a ban with no working unban is irreversible-by-accident.

recent_decisions.decision: Data-driven roles system (docs/design/roles-system.md). New `roles` SQLite table seeded with 5 built-ins (unverified/verified/donor/mod/admin); each role has can_stream/can_upload/can_voice capabilities + a base_tier that inherits the existing server_settings per-tier numeric limits (avoids a big limit migration; R4 consolidates later). Capability composition: effective_can_X = server_master_X_toggle && role.can_X. Assignment via the chat user-modal Role dropdown (set_user_role WS, generalizes mod_action mod/unmod). Built-in roles: caps editable, id/trust/base_tier locked, undeletable. Shipped R1 (v0.239 backend), R2 (v0.241 client parse + dropdown + data-driven badges), R3 (v0.242 Server Settings roles editor). R4 (move per-role limits into the roles table, deprecate base_tier) deferred until per-custom-role limits are actually wanted.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator wanted custom ranks + 'my lil bro big bro dad etc should be able to stream to our vps relay' WITHOUT promoting them to mod/admin. Chose (via AskUserQuestion) fully data-driven roles + per-role stream/upload/voice + role-dropdown assignment. Per infinite-of-x: roles are data not code. BEHAVIOR CHANGE shipped in v0.239: admin streaming now also requires the server-wide video_streaming_enabled master toggle (default OFF; old start path ignored it) — operator was told to flip it on in Server policy.

recent_decisions.decision: Clipboard image paste (Ctrl+V) MUST be detected at the raw winit layer (src/lib.rs window_event), NOT through egui's input. egui-winit intercepts the Cut/Copy/Paste shortcuts, reads clipboard TEXT only, and returns early WITHOUT emitting the key event — so for an image-only clipboard egui's input has zero signal. Took 3 attempts (v0.232 post-TextEdit, v0.233 top-of-draw — both read egui input, both failed; v0.234 winit-layer flag — works). The bug class is confined to exactly Cut/Copy/Paste; audited all of src/, no other instances. Future shortcut handling that competes with egui-winit's swallowed shortcuts must use the winit layer.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: print-screen then Ctrl+V worked on web but not the native app. The architectural lesson (egui-winit swallows C/X/V) is the durable takeaway — captured in lib.rs comments + this journal so it isn't rediscovered.

recent_decisions.decision: Split items toxicology: ingredients hold their own tox in sidecar RON files; items are pure ingredient lists with versioned `history` array. Toxicology derived at query time from the current ingredient list.
recent_decisions.rationale: User asked: 'wouldn't it be better to just list the item and ingredients and then from that list of ingredients determines toxicology' + 'we also want to make sure we accommodate the fact that the ingredients list might change for something like Oreos.' recent_decisions.decision: BUG-034 fixed in v0.124.0. `.github/workflows/build-desktop.yml` now publishes raw binaries (`HumanityOS-<platform>.exe` for Windows, plain executable for Linux/macOS) alongside the existing `.tar.gz` bundles. `src/updater.rs::find_platform_asset` prefers raw binary assets and refuses archive-only releases (returns 'No binary for this platform') instead of silently writing gzip bytes as a corrupt exe.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported in-app updater on Windows produced a binary that failed to launch with 'Unsupported 16-Bit Application'. Investigation: corrupted file was 38612146 bytes — exactly the size of `HumanityOS-windows-x64.tar.gz`. The updater downloaded the only release asset (a tar.gz bundle) and renamed it to .exe without extracting. `file` on the corrupted exe confirms gzip magic bytes. Two-part fix: workflow ships both formats; updater prefers raw.

recent_decisions.decision: Audit Session 3 (Tier 1.2 + Tier 4) lands alongside the BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0. Storage tests added: 7 for `dms.rs` (DM persistence, E2EE ciphertext preservation, direction-symmetric load, conversation isolation, mark-read, ECDH requires registered name, limit returns recent N) and 10 for `trading.rs` (escrow happy-path, complete requires both confirms, complete idempotent, item changes reset confirmations, cancelled cannot complete, third-party cannot confirm, cannot confirm pending, rejected→cancelled, unknown side rejected, list filters by party). Total lib tests: 173 → 190. Tier 4 dual-UI parity table added to `docs/design/ui-system.md`.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 1.2 — msg_handlers.rs et al had 0 tests over 3081 LOC. Storage-layer tests are the right scope (handler tests would need a heavy RelayState fixture). The new tests cover the load-bearing escrow correctness invariants the audit flagged.

recent_decisions.decision: BUG-035 + BUG-036 fixed in v0.125.0. (035) Native chat reply disappears after WS blip — fixed by no-op'ing channel-switch when the click target equals the active channel (chat.rs, 5 sites) and adding chat_sent_timestamps dedup to the HTTP history fetch (lib.rs). (036) Deleted system channels resurrect on every relay restart — fixed by gating the seed list with a `default_channels_seeded` row in server_state, with a one-shot migration that respects existing operators' channel sets when message_count > 0.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported a reply going missing in #general after a connection blip, plus channel-deletes via the cog modal coming back. Investigation: chat_messages.clear() ran on every click (even same-channel re-clicks); the HTTP history fetch had no dedup; src/relay/mod.rs re-seeded 6 system channels on every restart and INSERT OR IGNORE happily re-inserted previously-deleted rows. Both bugs documented in docs/BUGS.md as BUG-035 and BUG-036 with full root-cause traces.

recent_decisions.decision: Distribution-sovereignty plan documented in `docs/distribution-mirrors.md`. Recommended adoption order: (1) self-hosted Forgejo on VPS, (2) Codeberg mirror, (3) VPS release-binary mirror, (4) self-hosted BitTorrent tracker + seeder for data/asset payload, (5) Internet Archive seeder, (6) Software Heritage archival, (7) WinGet manifest. Steps 8+ scope-driven (IPFS, F-Droid/Flathub/Snap, Homebrew, Radicle, paid IPFS pinning).
recent_decisions.rationale: User wants HOS independent of GitHub, prefers self-hosting + torrent/magnet links to fit the P2P federation ethos. Vision: small exe is the entry point; on first run it pulls data/+assets/ from the torrent network with VPS as always-on seeder; client devices become seeders too. The doc is curated — only platforms that are good fits are listed. GitLab, SourceForge, Bitbucket, NotABug, Chocolatey were considered and dropped.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 1 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.127.0: self-hosted Forgejo at https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity. Forgejo v15.0.0 binary at /usr/local/bin/forgejo; user `forgejo`; data at /var/lib/forgejo; SQLite3; nginx reverse proxy 127.0.0.1:3000; Let's Encrypt TLS auto-renewing; self-registration disabled. Justfile `_commit` recipe now pushes to BOTH `origin` (GitHub) and `forge` (Forgejo); forge push is best-effort (`-` prefix in just) so a transient Forgejo outage doesn't block ship. Tags pushed to both remotes too (GitHub Actions Build Desktop App still keys off origin tag). Initial mirror: 12386 objects, 50 tags (v0.1.0 → v0.126.0), 2 branches (main + claude/happy-panini), 51 MB. `docs/forgejo-setup.md` documents the install + reproduce steps.
recent_decisions.rationale: Sovereignty layer is the keystone of the distribution-mirrors plan. Once forge holds the source, subsequent steps (release-binary mirror, BitTorrent seeder, Internet Archive backup, etc.) build on it. Two issues hit during install: Debian 11's git 2.30.2 too old for Forgejo (needed ≥2.34.1) — fixed by building 2.45.2 from source at /usr/local/bin/git; app.ini owned root:forgejo prevented the installer from saving config — fixed by chown forgejo:forgejo. Both documented in forgejo-setup.md so future operators don't repeat them.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 3 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.128.0: self-hosted release-binary mirror at https://united-humanity.us/releases/. nginx /releases/ location with autoindex; backfilled v0.122.0–v0.127.0; build-desktop.yml CI scp's binaries to VPS on every tag push via appleboy/scp-action; mirror-manifest job regenerates manifest.json + latest symlink via /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest. Step 2 (Codeberg) skipped temporarily — operator awaiting signup confirmation email; will land in a follow-up release.
recent_decisions.rationale: The auto-updater (BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0) will gain this as a fallback URL later — for now the mirror is a working independent download path, anyone can wget from it without GitHub. The manifest at /releases/manifest.json + the latest symlink mean operators don't need to know specific version numbers; clients can fetch latest deterministically. nginx sites-enabled/humanity was a regular file (not a symlink to sites-available) — edits had to go directly to sites-enabled. Documented inline in the orchestrator notes.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.128.2 shipped a CI fix: per-matrix mirror step replaced with a single Linux mirror-to-vps job using actions/download-artifact + appleboy/scp-action. v0.128.3 followed with continue-on-error on the upload step + if: always() on the regen step, because scp-action sometimes returns a false-positive i/o timeout AFTER the upload completes successfully. Workflow is now self-healing.
recent_decisions.rationale: Original v0.128.0 workflow used appleboy/scp-action per-matrix-job, but Docker container actions only run on Linux runners — macOS/Windows runners failed with 'Container action is only supported on Linux'. v0.128.2 consolidated the upload into one Linux job. v0.128.3 added tolerance for the action's tendency to return false-positive timeouts after successful uploads. Both fixes verified end-to-end in CI.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.129.0: BitTorrent seeder + magnet URIs. transmission-daemon installed on VPS (RPC localhost-only, peer port 51413/tcp+udp). /usr/local/bin/torrent-create-and-seed script creates .torrent files with 5 public trackers + WebSeed pointing back at the HTTPS mirror, then adds them to transmission. /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest extended to a two-phase script: (1) seed any unseeded .tar.gz, (2) build manifest with magnet URIs. Backfilled 36 torrents covering v0.122.0–v0.128.3 (~1.4 GB seeding). docs/torrent-infrastructure.md documents the full setup.
recent_decisions.rationale: Matches the user's original P2P architecture vision: VPS as always-on seed; client devices become seeders too via swarm participation. WebSeed makes the critical path tracker-independent — even with zero peers and every public tracker down, BitTorrent clients fall back to plain HTTPS from the VPS. CI required NO workflow change because the regen script now handles both seeding and manifest generation. Self-hosted tracker (opentracker) deferred — not blocking because of WebSeed.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4.5 landed in v0.130.0: layered packages + file-level manifest. New scripts/gen-data-manifest.js walks data/ + assets/icons/ + assets/shaders/, writes data-manifest-<tag>.json with per-file SHA-256 hash + Forgejo per-file URL (https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity/raw/tag/<tag>/<path>). bundle-data CI job now generates the manifest + uploads it as a release asset + as a workflow artifact. mirror-to-vps job depends on bundle-data so it picks up the data archive + manifest via download-artifact. Backfilled 10 data archives + 10 data manifests for v0.122.0–v0.129.0. transmission auto-seeded 10 new data-archive torrents (50 total now, ~3.6 GB).
recent_decisions.rationale: User instinct: 'shipping 38 MB for a 4 KB recipe edit is wasteful, and we should be able to inspect what's changing'. The layered architecture (binary / data / assets as separate torrents) plus file-level manifest enables future delta sync: a 4 KB data tweak becomes a 4 KB update. Forgejo's raw-file URLs serve as the per-file CDN — no IPFS or extra storage layer required. SHA-256 chosen over BLAKE3 to avoid extra deps in the manifest generator (Node stdlib only). Client-side delta sync UI (Step 4.6+) will read this manifest, compare to local files, and fetch only what changed.

recent_decisions.decision: Gameplay loop arc shipped v0.159 → v0.172 (14 minor releases). World ticks 24/7, 6 NPCs per room with dialog/greetings/wander, ambient chatter every 45s. PERSIST_KEY bumped 8 times (v1 → v8). 196 tests passing. New pages: Testing (QA loop), Browser (curated bookmarks). New nav: two-tier preview (Reality/Sim/Tools/Settings), opt-in via [▤], persists.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator was AFK and asked Claude to 'keep developing'. Each release ships independently testable changes with QA tasks added to data/testing/qa_tasks.json (now 25 entries). Quest framework is data-driven (chain_next_quest matches on quest id, easily extensible). Live VPS validation each release confirmed real-world behavior. Bot demo shows complete gameplay loop in 8 seconds — operators see the system working end-to-end without manual play.

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d arc shipped v0.208 → v0.212 (5 minor releases). Closes all of the operator's explicitly-stated requests from 2026-05-12: time controls (play/pause/scrub/Now/speed presets/reverse + Howard Hinnant date conversion, no chrono dep), pill-style body labels with collision-dodging + expand-to-card, live conjunction + solar eclipse detection from Earth's POV with visual highlights + HUD readout, Lagrange-point overlay (L1-L5 for Sun-Earth/Earth-Moon/Sun-Mars/Sun-Jupiter/Sun-Saturn with notable-parking tooltips JWST/Trojans/Queqiao etc.), reference-orbit rings (LEO/MEO/GEO/HEO + Lunar TLI for Earth + areostationary/Jovo-stationary/NRHO for Mars/Jupiter/Moon). New GuiState fields: cosmos_sim_time_seconds + cosmos_sim_speed + cosmos_last_real_instant + cosmos_sim_time_initialized + cosmos_expanded_body + cosmos_show_lagrange + cosmos_show_reference_orbits. New helpers: compute_lagrange_points (cube-root-mass-ratio for L1/L2/L3 + Rodrigues for L4/L5) + detect_sky_events (pairwise angular separation O(11²)≈55 pairs per frame from Earth POV) + reference_orbits_for (const &[ReferenceOrbit] per body) + draw_reference_orbits (96-segment polylines) + draw_body_info_card (auto-positioning + auto-flip pill expand cards). Cosmos doc §17a-sexies updated to be a complete shipping log + roadmap. cosmos.rs grew from ~1500 to ~2100 lines.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator quoted: 'as close to real as possible' + 'could we add a way to watch the orbits play fast forward, rewind, and skip/seek along days/weeks/years/etc' + 'all the planets line up in real life as they are today on May 12, 2026 then we could see what it'd look like on a great conjunction' + 'experience solar eclipses on the Earth' + 'pill UI for body labels — planet sprite on left, name on right, click expands to info card' + (earlier) 'include stuff like lagrange points and ... geostationary orbit'. All explicitly named features shipped. Three remaining Phase 4 items deferred with rationale in cosmos doc: 4d-quad (umbra lat/lon + forward scan), 4b (sprite→mesh wgpu integration), 4g (FPS AR-glasses overlay, requires player-in-world).

recent_decisions.decision: GUI-FIRST CONFIGURABILITY mandate codified + first slice shipped. Operator directive: make as many options/configs reachable IN-APP (not CLI) -- for the operator, tech-illiterate users, AND AI agents (who can then enumerate available actions instead of guessing shell commands). Codified as a CLAUDE.md non-negotiable rule. Wrote docs/design/in-app-ops.md: north-star = data-driven admin ACTION REGISTRY (GUI renders + AI enumerates/invokes via list/run endpoints) + full audit of current CLI debt (alerts/backups/fail2ban/relay-control/secrets/health all SSH-only today) mapped to in-app homes + build order + guardrails. v0.287.0 shipped build-order slice #1: System Health panel -- get_admin_stats now returns a best-effort `system` object (version, disk via df, watchdog_state from /run file, newest-backup status), rendered as a color-cued panel in the web admin dashboard (admin.html + admin-app.js). Verified live: relay returns it, data sources (df 15%, watchdog 'up') confirmed on VPS. NEXT in track: native Server Settings parity for System Health (operator primarily uses native app), then first WRITE panel (alert-channels editor), then backups/federation panels, then factor out the action registry + AI list/run endpoints.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator stated this 3 ways (their preference + accessibility mission + AI-operability) = it's a first-class principle, so made it permanent (CLAUDE.md rule) not just remembered. Built the lowest-risk read-only slice first to prove the path + immediately kill SSH-for-status, per the documented build order. The action-registry north star is the elegant end state (infinite-of-x: actions as data) but earned incrementally -- factor it out after 3-4 panels exist, not speculatively now.

recent_decisions.decision: TIER 1 CLOSED. Operator decisions: fail2ban over Cloudflare (DDoS), skip off-box monitor, plan federation. Shipped: v0.286.2 configurable multi-channel external alerting (scripts/humanity-alert.js: ntfy/Discord/Telegram/webhook, admin-opt-in via data/alert-channels.secrets.json, wired into watchdog + disk-guard, anti-spam, dry-run-verified on VPS). v0.286.3 fail2ban nginx jails (scripts/fail2ban/nginx.local: nginx-limit-req + nginx-botsearch, conservative + ignoreip loopback/private, installed live + version-controlled in deploy.yml; sshd jail was already active + SSH is key-only so no lockout risk; 3 jails verified active, 0 bans, relay+SSH fine) + federation activation DESIGN DOC (docs/design/federation-activation.md: trust model, abuse-model table, 4-phase plan; key finding = federation already fail-closed at trust_tier 0 so dormant is safe; impl moved to TIER 2 #1). TIER 1 scorecard: #1 DDoS done (fail2ban), #2 alerting VPS-side done + off-box skipped, #3 SQLite corruption done (v0.286.0), #4 federation design done/impl->TIER2, #5 crash-loop done (watchdog). Next code work: TIER 2 (federation phases, or native voice/streaming/trade) or TIER 3 (ELI5 tooltips, onboarding, a11y) — operator's pick.
recent_decisions.rationale: All TIER 1 code-actionable work shipped autonomously; the 2 decision-gated items resolved by operator. fail2ban done carefully (filters tested against real logs before enabling, ignoreip + key-only SSH means no lockout, moderate bantimes so a mistake self-recovers). Federation: design-first per operator, and the doc surfaces the two real unknowns (moderation propagation + profile-gossip rate limit) so Phase 3's second-VPS test is the decision point before any third-party peer.

recent_decisions.decision: TIER 0 CLOSED + unattended-operation safety net, while operator stepped away. (1) PRODUCTION INCIDENT recovered: relay was crash-looping ~25 min — disk hit 92% (cause: /var/www/humanity/releases/ had 287 unrotated release dirs = 91 GB), disk-guard correctly reclaimed target/ but no deploy followed so the binary was gone. Recovered by: cleaning 277 old release dirs (freed 91 GB, disk 91%->13%), pushing v0.283.3 to trigger CI rebuild. DISCOVERED during recovery: pre-built release binaries are GLIBC 2.35 / OpenSSL 3.x (GitHub Actions Ubuntu) and CANNOT run on the Debian-11 VPS (GLIBC 2.31) — the relay ALWAYS rebuilds from source on the VPS; pre-built artifacts are for end users only. ALSO discovered + fixed: the on-VPS backup script (/usr/local/bin/humanity-backup-db) pointed at the dead pre-v0.90 path /opt/Humanity/crates/humanity-relay/data/relay.db and had been backing up an auto-created EMPTY db for over a month (fossils moved to backups/fossil-pre-v0.90/). (2) Durable fixes v0.283.4: disk-guard now rotates /var/www/humanity/releases to last 10 versions; backup script in-repo (scripts/humanity-backup-db.sh) + deploy installs it. (3) Off-site backup SHIPPED (v0.283.5, device-mesh stopgap): operator PC pulls relay DB every 6h via scheduled task 'HumanityOS Relay Backup Pull' (scripts/backup-relay-from-vps.ps1); full vision in docs/design/device-mesh.md. (4) BUG-037 fixed (v0.284.0): chat messages duplicated in-memory after a WS-reconnect history re-fetch — added content-based dedup (sender_key,timestamp_ms) to both append paths. (5) v0.285.0: deleted the stale GitHub webhook (dead ngrok URL) + made /api/github-webhook fail-closed (was a forged-announcement vector). The webhook was NEVER the update-autoposter — that's the CI Deploy Bot via /api/send+API_SECRET, separate + healthy. (6) v0.285.2: nginx /health route added (public was 404; off-site monitoring needs it) — TIER 0 final item; nginx config captured to scripts/nginx/humanity.conf (was VPS-only, bus-factor gap); relay watchdog (scripts/humanity-relay-watchdog.sh + timer, every 2 min) — HTTP-liveness + self-heal restart + recovery announcement, catches alive-but-hung + systemd-gave-up that Restart=always misses, logs CRITICAL if binary missing (won't rebuild unattended). (7) v0.286.0: Storage::open_resilient — boot-time integrity check + restore-newest-healthy-backup + quarantine-corrupt + refuse-to-start-if-no-good-backup (safe failure, no silent wipe); 4 tests; relay verified booting clean on it. All TIER 0 done; PRIORITIES.md current.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator asked to 'wrap up tier 0' + 'what all can you do at once so I can step away for a long while'. Sequenced for unattended safety: watchdog (the self-healing net) deployed BEFORE the riskier boot-path change, so anything subsequent had a catch. Verified relay health after every deploy. Failed-loud-over-silent-wipe in open_resilient matches the operator's consistent no-surprise-data-loss preference. The big lesson chain (release-mirror bloat -> disk-guard -> missing binary -> crash-loop, plus the fossil-backup discovery, plus the GLIBC mismatch) is all in docs/INCIDENT-PLAYBOOK.md so it isn't rediscovered.

recent_decisions.decision: Inc6 attended wipe CONFIRMED COMPLETE via read-only SQL inventory on live VPS. Operator asked 'didn't we do the wipe yesterday?' — confirmed: channels=2 (fresh-schema defaults general+announcements), bulk tables empty (listings/trades/proposals/federated_servers/banned/muted/profiles/uploads all 0), registered_names=3 (Shaostoul+Brave+Deploy Bot, all post-Inc3 Dilithium pubkeys), messages=920 (#announcements 919 = 888 archive re-seed + 31 post-wipe deploy bot, #general 1 = Brave's 'test' 2026-05-19 21:11:10Z), direct_messages=4 (real PQ-sealed envelopes Shaostoul+Brave). The DesktopUser_NNNN identities visible in the operator's earlier screenshot were ephemeral test connections that never claimed names (hence no registered_names rows). recent_decisions.decision: Post-cutover hardening sweep CLOSED. Operator: 'How can the current system fail? Let's work through fixing the problems.' Six tasks created from the v0.266.x independent security review + the threat-model self-audit; five shipped this session, one deferred for UX. v0.274.0 Inc3b: two-phase identify with Dilithium nonce challenge — relay refuses to bind a socket to a public_key the client cannot prove possession of. Preimage `hum/identify/v1\n{nonce_hex}\n{pubkey_hex}` (UTF-8); pending_identify state struct per-socket; IdentifyChallenge/IdentifyResponse RelayMessage variants; verify_dilithium_b64 helper; inc3b_tests::identify_challenge_sign_verify_roundtrip (5 cases: happy, tampered preimage, wrong key, corrupt sig, bad b64). Bot fastpath unchanged (skips challenge, auths via bot_secret). Closes HIGH-2. v0.275.0 MED-1: 4 native chat-send sites now stamp pq_signature via net::identity::pq_sign_chat(seed, content, ts); relay's chat verify gate flipped to absent->reject for non-bot senders. Native chat no longer unsigned. v0.275.0 pq-wipe.sh hardening: Gate 1 (--yes), Gate 2 (pgrep refuses concurrent 'cargo build .. --features relay'; closes 2026-05-19 race where disk-guard rm'd target/ mid-rebuild and 888 sqlite no-such-table errors followed), Gate 3 (refuses if relay binary missing), 30s schema-readiness poll for messages+channels tables (was bare sleep 4), seed-verify count==archive-length check. v0.276.0: federation profile gossip verify_profile_signature switched Ed25519->Dilithium3. Was the last Ed25519-signed identity path that referenced the user's public_key field — which has been Dilithium hex since Inc3 — so the old verify was forgeable PQ and would have silently rejected every signed gossip post-Inc3. Test fixtures rebuilt on DilithiumKeypair::from_seed + derive_dilithium_seed; all 8 federation tests pass. sign_with_server_key (server-to-server FederationHello) kept Ed25519 by design — separate trust path, server's own key. v0.277.0: native vault PBKDF2 bumped 100_000->600_000 iters to match web (the web sandbox is the harder target; asymmetry was backwards). AppConfig + GuiState gain key_iterations: u32 (serde default 100_000 for pre-v0.277 configs). encrypt_private_key always stamps PBKDF2_ITERATIONS_NEW; decrypt_private_key takes iterations as a parameter; defensive clamp to legacy floor if iterations==0 (corruption defense). Silent migration: Unlock path re-encrypts legacy 100k vaults to 600k after the user proves they know the passphrase — one-time per vault, no UX prompt. Change-passphrase auto-upgrades by virtue of re-encrypting. 6 pbkdf2_migration_tests: new encrypts use 600k, legacy decrypts with stored iters, new round-trips, wrong iter fails, full 100k->600k round-trip, corrupt iters=0 clamps. CLAUDE.md crypto table updated for all four. Inc5c-tail (#3) deferred — 3 standalone pages (settings-app.js vault sync, admin-app.js admin_stats, market-app.js listing reviews) still Ed25519-sign relay auth; they don't share state with the chat client, so fixing requires re-prompting passphrase per page or moving features into chat shell — needs operator product call. All six security-review findings: HIGH-1 v0.267.0, HIGH-2 v0.274.0, MED-1 v0.275.0, LOW-1 documented; federation gossip + PBKDF2 are above-and-beyond closes from the threat-model self-audit.

recent_decisions.decision: Operator chose TRUE P2P GROUPS (via AskUserQuestion) over relay-mediated-fix or federated-fallback. Wrote docs/design/p2p-groups.md: a group becomes a signed object + append-only signed membership/message logs replicated peer-to-peer over the EXISTING WebRTC DataChannels (web/chat/chat-p2p.js); relays demoted to optional accelerators (signaling rendezvous / opportunistic cache / presence). Invite = signed connection TICKET (not a relay URL). E2EE via per-epoch group key (generalize the Kyber768 dual-seal in src/net/dm_pq.rs), re-keyed on membership change for forward secrecy. Grounded via a research agent: reuse chat-p2p DataChannels, voice-room N-mesh signaling template, signed contact card (relay-free bootstrap), signed_objects+gossip+latest-wins, dual-seal, applySyncBundle merge, signed_moderation_logs governance shape. THE core gap = relay-independent signaling (today webrtc_signal rides ONE relay, relay.rs ~987/5507) -> multi-relay failover + peer-assisted signaling + TURN/peer-relay for NAT. Phases P1 sovereign-data+working-ticket-invite (fixes the 404) -> P2 signed+E2EE msgs -> P3 P2P transport -> P4 relay-independence (the payoff) -> P5 mDNS/DHT. Added as TIER 2 #8. NOT started code — design awaits operator review of 3 open decisions (TURN, epoch-key vs per-msg KEM, relay-as-accelerator).
recent_decisions.why: Operator major concern: if united-humanity.us is down, can people still join groups via a shared URL? Investigation: no — groups fully relay-dependent today. Operator picked the resilience north star (true P2P). Per do-it-right/no-bandaids + the design-before-code rule, the correct first increment is a grounded design doc that BUILDS ON existing primitives, not immediate code.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups: locked the 3 open decisions (operator approved my recs) — TURN = operator-run + peer-as-TURN fallback; encryption = per-epoch group key (re-key on membership change, forward secrecy); relay = optional accelerator. Marked DECIDED in docs/design/p2p-groups.md. Then shipped Phase 1 slice (a) v0.292.0: the relay-side SOVEREIGN DATA MODEL. New module src/relay/storage/groups_p2p.rs defines the object-format spec (module doc-comment, the cross-language contract) for group_v1 (group_id = its own object_id, self-certifying) / group_member_v1 (append-only admit/remove, references group_id, Phase-1 creator-only authority) / group_msg_v1 (Phase 2, encrypted). index_group + index_group_member project into new p2p_groups + p2p_group_roster tables (the membership-log fold), wired into put_signed_object. Additive — the existing relay-mediated group path (social.rs/handlers) is untouched, so nothing breaks during migration. 3 tests pass incl. the security test that an admit signed by a NON-creator is rejected (valid signature, failed authorization). Built on the existing signed_objects substrate (canonical CBOR + BLAKE3 object_id + Dilithium3 via core::object::ObjectBuilder) — ZERO new crypto.
recent_decisions.why: Operator said move forward with the plan + greenlit starting Phase 1. Per do-it-right + the codebase contract-first convention (object_format.md), the first slice is the data model with the spec embedded as code doc + tests, additive so it cannot break the working path. Reused the signed-object framework rather than inventing — the design is implementable with no new crypto, just new object types + projections.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (b) shipped v0.292.1: cross-language signed-object construction for the web. Found the real gap — the web had Dilithium signing + BLAKE3 but NO canonical-CBOR encoder, so it literally could not build a signed_object (blocked web votes/vouches/recovery too, all marked follow-up). Built web/shared/canonical-cbor.js (byte-exact match to src/relay/core/encoding.rs: RFC7049 length-first key sort, shortest-int headers, definite-length, no floats/tags) + web/shared/pq-object.js (buildSignedObject / buildGroupV1 / buildGroupMemberV1 producing the POST /api/v2/objects submission with zero-sig-placeholder signable bytes matching Rust signable_bytes). KAT-LOCKED: scripts/group-object-kat.mjs (just group-kat) vs groups_p2p.rs::group_v1_canonical_kat assert identical payload hex (a1646e616d65696b61742d67726f7570) + object_id (c909a8df...70f7db9b) for a fixed group_v1 input. JS matched Rust byte-for-byte on the first try. End-to-end chain now ready: web builds group_v1 -> POST /api/v2/objects (route exists, api_v2_objects::post_object) -> put_signed_object verifies + index_group projects to roster. Patch bump — the Rust change is #[cfg(test)] only, binary unchanged.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: get this done completely and properly. Per the codebase contract-first + KAT convention (pq-kat.mjs), the foundation for any web-built signed object is a canonical-CBOR encoder proven byte-identical to Rust — without it a web group object is unverifiable by the relay. Built generic (not group-specific) so it unblocks all web signed objects.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (c) — invite + admission model (v0.293.0). Design call (dictated by the resilience goal, not asked): invite = creator-signed CAPABILITY with a secret commitment, so members join even when the creator is OFFLINE. group_invite_v1 (creator-signed, references group, payload {expires_at, secret_hash=BLAKE3(secret)}); the out-of-band ticket carries the secret; group_join_v1 (joiner-signed, references [group_id, invite_id], payload {secret}); the roster fold admits the join AUTHOR iff BLAKE3(secret)==invite.secret_hash AND now<expires_at AND the invite was creator-signed. Randos cannot join (no secret); expired invites rejected; invites from non-creators are not projected so joins against them fail. Relay: index_group_invite/index_group_join + p2p_group_invites table in groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object; 7 tests pass. Web: buildGroupInviteV1/buildGroupJoinV1 + encodeInviteTicket/decodeInviteTicket + randomInviteSecret in pq-object.js (uses the KAT-locked canonical encoder). max_uses deferred (needs coordination; expiry is coordination-free). Additive/dormant until the UI (d) creates these objects.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: do it completely + properly. Offline-join is required by the mission (relay/creator down should not block joining); a capability+secret-commitment invite is the standard correct pattern and avoids needing the admin online to admit. Expiry chosen over use-count for Phase 1 because expiry is deterministic/coordination-free across peers.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (d) v0.294.0 — chat UI create+join flow; THE 404 IS FIXED. Relay: GET /api/v2/groups?pubkey=<hex> (api_v2_objects::my_p2p_groups) reads p2p_groups+roster projection (p2p_groups_for_member computes the fp from pubkey). Web: new chat-groups-p2p.js (IIFE, lazy dynamic-imports /shared/pq-object.js + the vendored noble bundle for blake3, signs with the chat identity via window.pqSignMessage(myDilithiumSecret,...)): createP2pGroup -> buildGroupV1 -> POST /api/v2/objects; createP2pInvite -> buildGroupInviteV1(7-day) -> ticket via encodeInviteTicket (shown in a copy dialog); joinP2pGroupByTicket -> decodeInviteTicket -> buildGroupJoinV1; loadP2pGroups -> GET. chat-social.js renderGroupList now renders P2P groups (click -> openP2pGroupDialog roster+invite) above legacy relay-mediated ones; promptCreateGroup/promptJoinGroup repointed to the P2P flow (old group_create/group_join WS path retired in the UI; legacy groups still render until migrated). index.html loads chat-groups-p2p.js after chat-social.js. NOT browser-tested (preview unavailable) but: relay compiles, KATs+tests pass, the submission shape matches Rust SignedObjectSubmission field-for-field, signable bytes use the same zero-sig placeholder + KAT-locked encoder, and signing reuses the proven identify Dilithium path — so create/POST/verify/project is sound by construction. Minor bump (relay binary changed: new route + read query).
recent_decisions.why: Operator: do it completely + properly, dont stop for length. (d) is where groups become usable again + the invite 404 dies. Messaging deferred to Phase 2 (E2EE) per the agreed phasing — a Phase-1 P2P group shows roster + invite, no chat yet. recent_decisions.decision: Operator deleted the live legacy test group + left for a few hours: instructed work on what you can. So (e) migration is MOOT (no live group to migrate); retiring the legacy group code path waits until the operator verifies the new (d) UI (keep it as fallback). Proceeding AUTONOMOUSLY on PHASE 2 (E2EE group messages) — the agreed next track, encryption scheme already decided (per-epoch group key). Safe to build unsupervised because the relay side is store+serve OPAQUE ciphertext + membership-gate (relay never decrypts), additive/dormant (no client uses the new message path until the chat UI is wired, which is deferred until the operator can browser-verify). Phase 2 needs no new crypto: reuse dm_pq.rs (ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM) to seal one epoch key to each member, AES-GCM messages under it. Member Kyber pubs come from registered_names.kyber_public via get_kyber_public(dilithium_hex) (already exists) — enrich GET /api/v2/groups roster with kyber_pub so the web sender can seal.
recent_decisions.why: Operator away + work-on-what-you-can. The UI wiring (which needs browser verification) is deferred to when the operator is back.

recent_decisions.decision: PHASE 2 slice 1 (autonomous) — relay E2EE-group-message backend. New object types projected (groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object): group_epoch_key_v1 (creator-authorized; p2p_group_epochs table records which object holds epoch N) + group_msg_v1 (membership-gated via p2p_group_has_member; p2p_group_messages log). The relay stores/serves OPAQUE ciphertext only — it never decrypts (each member key copy is ML-KEM-sealed in the payload; messages AES-GCM under the epoch key). Read endpoints (api_v2_objects.rs): GET /api/v2/groups/{id}/members (roster + each member kyber_public from registered_names, for the sender to seal to), /messages (encrypted history, capped 200), /epoch (latest group_epoch_key_v1). 2 new tests (epoch_key_indexed_only_from_creator, only_member_messages_are_logged) — 9 groups_p2p tests pass. Additive/dormant — no client posts these yet; safe to ship unsupervised.
recent_decisions.why: Operator away + work-on-what-you-can. Relay-side E2EE message plumbing is the safe high-value autonomous slice: relay never decrypts (E2EE), membership-gated, additive, tested. The client crypto (seal epoch key to members, encrypt/decrypt) is slice 2; the chat UI (needs browser verification) is slice 3, deferred until the operator is back.

New: canonical-cbor.js decodeCanonicalCbor (parse the relay payloads back); pq-object.js randomEpochKey, buildGroupEpochKeyV1 (seals one 32-byte key to each members Kyber pub via the proven dm_pq scheme, payload = {epoch, recipients:[{fp,ek_ct,nonce,ct}]}), openGroupEpochKey (find my recipient entry + decapsulate via window.pqDmOpen), aesGcmEncrypt/Decrypt (crypto.subtle AES-GCM), buildGroupMsgV1 (payload {epoch, nonce, ct}), parseGroupMsgPayload. chat-groups-p2p.js: createP2pGroup now auto-issues an initial epoch_key sealed to the creator (so chat works immediately for the creator); openP2pGroupDialog rewritten into a real chat view — fetches the latest epoch key + decrypts message history + renders, compose box (Enter to send) encrypts via AES-GCM and POSTs group_msg_v1, polls every 4s while open. The relay never sees plaintext (Phase 2 backend in v0.295.0 already stores opaque ciphertext + serves it through /messages and /epoch endpoints). KAT still locks (encoder unchanged). No Rust changes — patch bump. Native chat-in-groups is deferred (the operator can verify the full loop on web). Re-key on join is deferred (the create-time epoch only covers the creator; cross-identity tests need a manual Rotate-key button — Phase-2 follow-up). Phases 3-5 are resilience tracks (P2P transport, relay-independence, mDNS/DHT) — not blocking testing of the user-facing feature.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: finish the phases so I can test properly. The user-facing testable goal is Phase 2 (E2EE chat in groups). Phases 3-5 are resilience tracks (the system survives a relay outage / runs without any server) which dont visibly change what a single user can do during a normal test on a working relay. Shipping web Phase 2 unlocks the full create -> invite -> join -> chat loop in self-test on the operators existing setup. Cross-identity testing needs re-key-on-join; native chat-in-groups needs more native UI work; both flagged as follow-ups.

Cursor switches to pointing-hand on hover. Cargo check --features native passes. Relay binary unchanged (all edits feature-gated to native).
